# Sean's Workspace

> A long-lived, surface/device/LLM-agnostic personal operating environment for design,
> engineering, product, data, and game work. It is simultaneously a git repository (the
> source of truth), an Obsidian vault, and an agent workspace. Any capable model — not just
> one vendor's — can enter, orient, and work here by following the universal contract below.
> Nothing here depends on Google Drive, Desktop Commander, or a specific assistant.

## Start here (read in order)
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the universal agent contract — folder semantics, read order, the skill
  loading-precedence algorithm, and the bootstrap-on-a-checkout protocol.
- [skills.registry.json](02-skills/skills.registry.json): machine-readable skill graph (tiers,
  prerequisites, cross-references, triggers, precomputed load chains). Generated from frontmatter.
- [workspace-ontology.md](01-shared-references/workspace-ontology.md): vocabulary + the
  "where does this belong?" routing map. Read before writing anything.

## How to work here
- Resolve the workspace root as the directory containing `AGENTS.md`. The git checkout is the
  source of truth — read and write plain files; commit reviewable diffs.
- To answer a request: match it to skills (via `triggers`/`description`), then load the skill plus
  its `load_chains` ancestors in order (foundation → hub → spoke). See AGENTS.md for the algorithm.
- **Multi-agent continuity:** this is one unified contract for *every* agent (not just Claude/Cursor/
  Perplexity). On entry, read the active project's `SESSION-STATE.md` **Live handoff** block to pick up
  exactly where the last agent left off; on handoff/pause/end, update it + append an attributed
  `session-log` entry. See AGENTS.md → "Multi-agent continuity & handoff". A new agent needs no adapter.
- Before editing the workspace, consult the routing map and the contribution framework
  ([00-frameworks/08-workspace-contribution-framework.md](00-frameworks/08-workspace-contribution-framework.md)).

## Contracts + specs
- [skill-frontmatter.md](01-shared-references/skill-frontmatter.md): the SKILL.md frontmatter v2 spec.
- [skills.registry.schema.json](02-skills/skills.registry.schema.json): JSON Schema for the registry.
- [epistemic-standards.md](01-shared-references/epistemic-standards.md),
  [artifact-standards.md](01-shared-references/artifact-standards.md): reasoning + output standards.

## Per-tool adapters (thin layers over AGENTS.md; optional)
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) — Claude Code / Desktop · [CURSOR.md](CURSOR.md) — Cursor ·
  [PERPLEXITY.md](PERPLEXITY.md) — Perplexity. A new tool needs no adapter to work at full fidelity;
  it just executes the AGENTS.md contract.

## Generate / validate
- `python3 09-tools/build-registry.py` — regenerate the skill registry from frontmatter (stdlib-only).
